hypothesis: If a 426M-parameter hybrid Transformer–Mamba-2 encoder–decoder pretrained with a multimodal BPE tokenizer on diverse molecular representations is used, then it will learn aligned, modality-invariant latent embeddings that yield better clustering (lower Davies–Bouldin and higher ARI/V‑Measure/Fowlkes–Mallows), more accurate inter-representation translation (higher validity, Tanimoto, BLEU, and Jaccard), and improved property-prediction performance across small molecules, polymers, and electrolytes than baselines because joint pretraining and cross-attention teach shared chemical semantics.
